concept.title: Access Control Lists (Firewall Filters)
concept.shortdesc: Read this topic to learn about Layer 2 access control lists (Firewall filters) in the cloud-native router.

section.title: Access Control Lists (Firewall Filters)
section.p: Starting with Juniper Cloud-Native Router Release 22.2 we've included a limited firewall filter capability. You can configure the filters using the Junos OS CLI within the cloud-native router controller, using NETCONF, or the cloud-native router APIs. Starting with Juniper Cloud-Native Router Release 23.2, you can also configure firewall filters using node annotations and custom configuration template at the time of Cloud-Native Router deployment. Please review the deployment guide for more details.

During deployment, the system defines and applies firewall filters to block traffic from passing directly between the router interfaces. You can dynamically define and apply more filters. Use the firewall filters to:

li.p: Define firewall filters for bridge family traffic.
Define filters based on one or more of the following fields: source MAC address, destination MAC address, or EtherType.
Define multiple terms within each filter.
Discard the traffic that matches the filter.

section.title: Troubleshooting
section.p: The following table lists some of the potential problems that you might face when you implement firewall rules or ACLs in the cloud-native router. You run most of these commands on the host server.

table.title: L2 Firewall Filter or ACL Troubleshooting

row.entry: Firewall filters or ACLs not working
gRPC connection (port 50052) to the vRouter is down. Check the gRPC connection.
The ui-pubd process is not running. Check whether ui-pubd is running.
Firewall filter or ACL show commands not working
The gRPC connection (port 50052) to the vRouter is down. Check the gRPC connection.
The firewall service is not running.
show log filter.logYou must run this command in the JCNR-controller (cRPD) CLI.
